items.summary: Godrej Seeds and Genetics Ltd has purchased office space across three floors in Vikhroli's Godrej One tower. The deal totals Rs 366 crore for over 70,000 sq ft. This intra-group transaction signifies a consolidation of real estate assets. The acquisition highlights a trend of large corporations centralizing office ownership. Vikhroli is a growing office hub in Mumbai's eastern corridor.

items.summary: U.S. stock index futures climbed on Wednesday after media reports that the U.S. was seeking a month-long ceasefire in its war with Iran allayed some investor fears around prolonged energy supply disruptions.

items.title: CCPA warns restaurants against levying gas, LPG charge
items.summary: The Central Consumer Protection Authority has issued a stern warning to hotels and restaurants. They are prohibited from adding extra charges such as 'LPG charges' or 'fuel cost recovery' to customer bills. These practices are now classified as unfair trade practices. Only menu prices and applicable taxes are permitted. Strict action will be taken against establishments that violate these directives.

items.title: Rural job scheme demand falls to 15.6 million people, a 5-year low for March as work rises in farms, urban centres
items.summary: The sharp drop in demand for the rural jobs scheme suggests a significant change in labour patterns. Experts believe better monsoon and a boom in manufacturing and urban construction are drawing workers away.

items.title: Gold prices rebound above Rs 1,44,000, silver near Rs 2,36,000: Why bullion is rising despite strong dollar, high interest rates
items.summary: Gold prices rebounded above Rs 1.44 lakh and silver hovered near Rs 2.36 lakh after a sharp correction, supported by a softer dollar, easing oil prices and value buying. Despite the recovery, strong US dollar and higher-for-longer interest rates continue to cap gains, keeping bullion volatile amid ongoing US–Iran uncertainty.

items.title: Gold surges 4% as ceasefire hopes drive India’s second straight ally
items.summary: The BSE Sensex gained 1,205 points or 1.63% to close at 75,273.45, while the Nifty 50 advanced 394 points or 1.72% to settle at 23,306.45

items.summary: New Bill proposes a ‘designated authority’, with powers of a civil court, to take charge of foreign contributions and assets created out of such funds if an organization’s registration is cancelled, surrendered, not renewed.

items.summary: India's healthcare sector is set for significant growth, aiming for USD 700 billion by 2030. However, financing remains a challenge with high out-of-pocket expenses. Experts emphasize public-private partnerships to transform healthcare delivery. India is emerging as a global healthcare service provider, offering world-class outcomes at lower costs. Addressing workforce and infrastructure needs is crucial for future progress.

items.summary: The Nifty 50 rose 1.72% today, taking the two-day rally to 3.5%. The rally was broad-based, as barring Coal India (down 2%) and Power Grid (up just 0.05%), all index constituents participated in the stock market rebound.

items.title: SpaceX plans to file for $75 billion IPO as soon as this week: Report
items.summary: SpaceX is aiming to file its initial public offering ​prospectus with regulators later this week ​or next week, The Information reported on Tuesday, ​citing a person with direct knowledge of the plans.

items.summary: Analysts say the rally in bullion was driven by improving sentiment in the global commodities market

items.summary: Domestic production has surged, driven by indigenous hybrid development and a strong public-private partnership, significantly reducing import dependence and saving foreign exchange.

items.summary: The stand, located in the North Pavilion Lower Bowl of the 30,000-seater venue, will have over 1,500 seats and serve as India’s first dedicated “Pink Stand” — a space designed for women and families

items.title: South tech hubs Bengaluru, Hyderabad reshape India's urban economy: Report
items.summary: Bengaluru and Hyderabad are powering more than half of India's urban growth. These tech hubs are reshaping jobs, consumption, and city futures. They account for over 50 percent of new office space added in top cities. Bengaluru leads in startups and innovation. Hyderabad is a growing Global Capability Centre hub. Both cities are key for entry-level hiring across major sectors.

items.title: Kuku bets on 'fourth wave' as audiences shift to bite-sized content
items.summary: Kuku, a Bengaluru-based company, is focusing on short, mobile-first content. It has expanded from audio to video and is entering cinema. Kuku's subscriber base has grown significantly. The company uses AI extensively for content creation, aiming to produce thousands of micro-dramas monthly. This strategy targets users' mobile consumption habits.

items.title: India set to lead in 6G technology, says Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia
items.summary: Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia said India is positioning itself to take global leadership in 6G technology, highlighting the country’s growing strength in telecom innovation and patent development.

items.title: India’s job market gains momentum with 4.7% workforce growth: Report
items.summary: According to the latest Employment Outlook Report from TeamLease Staffing, the projected Net Employment Change (NEC) marked a consistent upward trajectory from the 2.8 per cent and 4.4 per cent growth recorded over the previous two half-yearly periods to 4.7 per cent.

items.title: India's GDP forecast raised to 7.1% for FY27 as S&P Global flags oil risk, tech tailwinds
items.summary: India's economy shows robust growth, projected at 7.1% for FY27. Strong domestic demand and investments in technology are key drivers. Global geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East, pose a risk through potential oil price spikes. India's reliance on energy imports makes it vulnerable. The nation's growth trajectory will be tested against these external factors.

items.summary: The Indian stock market saw a significant rally on Wednesday. The Sensex jumped 1,205 points, closing at 75,273. Amidst this broader market strength, six stocks from the BSE 1000 index reached their highest levels in a year. These new 52-week highs signal positive momentum for these companies. Prime Focus, Acutaas Chemicals, and Aurobindo Pharma were among those reaching new peaks.

items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Indian equity benchmarks gained for a ​second session on Wednesday, as prospects of a Middle ‌East ceasefire pulled oil prices lower and eased concerns ​around growth in the world's third-biggest crude ⁠importer.

The scheme has a significant outlay to drive these developments.

items.summary: Electric mobility firm Matter Motor's CEO Mohal Lalbhai highlighted the transformative role of data and intelligent systems in the automotive industry. He explained that vehicles are evolving into software-defined entities, enabling regular updates, data-driven services, and new post-purchase revenue streams for manufacturers. This shift promises smarter, adaptive vehicles that learn and improve over time.

Market capitalisation of BSE-listed companies increased by Rs 15,78,165.17 crore, or about 3.80 per cent, over the past two sessions. The rally was broad-based, with buying seen across sectors.

items.title: Mutual Funds vs Traditional Investments: Which builds wealth faster? Experts explain
items.summary: When it comes to wealth creation, investors generally have to make a choice between mutual funds and traditional modes of investment. Speaking with Zee Business,  Akhil Chaturvedi, Director & Chief Business Officer, Motilal Oswal AMC, and Rishikesh Palve, Director at Anand Rathi Wealth, explained how mutual funds compare with traditional modes of investment.

Flights from Oman and Saudi Arabia are operational, with Qatar's airspace partially open. Special arrangements are in place for those in Kuwait and Bahrain, routing them via Saudi Arabia. Evacuations from Iran and travel from Israel through Jordan are also being managed.

items.summary: His mother, Chandrakala Gaur, was terminally ill, and he was granted interim bail on this ground earlier

items.summary: Global brokerage UBS has downgraded Indian equities to Neutral from Attractive due to escalating geopolitical risks in the Middle East and their impact on India's energy imports. Analysts highlight the nation's vulnerability to oil price spikes and supply disruptions, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Is OpenAI shutting down its viral AI video app?
items.summary: Is OpenAI shutting down Sora? Artificial intelligence research and development company OpenAI has announced that it is pulling the plug of its social media app — Sora — foreign media reported. It was a platform for sharing short videos created by artificial intelligence and became very popular last autumn.

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank faces Rs 150 crore Panchkula FD fraud allegations
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank is facing allegations of a Rs 150 crore fixed deposit fraud linked to the Panchkula Municipal Corporation, where discrepancies surfaced after a maturity request. The case, which follows closely after the IDFC First Bank episode in Haryana, involves claims of fake records, missing deposits and an alleged cover-up.

items.title: India’s packaging industry to reach $92 billion by FY30: Avendus
items.summary: India's packaging sector is set for robust expansion, projected to hit $92 billion by FY30. This growth is fueled by rising consumption and investor interest.  The industry is expected to outpace GDP growth, driven by demand from food, pharma, and e-commerce.  Rigid plastic packaging leads the charge, while paper packaging also sees gains.  This presents a strong opportunity for investors.

items.title: CMPDI IPO allotment: Did you receive Coal India subsidiary's shares? Check status, GMP, issue timeline
items.summary: Central Mine Planning & Design Institute Ltd (CMPDI) IPO share allotment is expected today. The Rs 1,842 crore Offer for Sale (OFS) saw full subscription, driven by Qualified Institutional Buyers. Investors can check their allotment status on BSE or the registrar's website.

items.summary: TVS Holdings has declared an interim dividend of Rs 86 per equity share, amounting to a 1,720% payout on its 2.02 crore shares. The record date for this dividend is April 2, with payments expected within 30 days. The company also raised Rs 650 crore through non-convertible debentures.

items.summary: Market volatility surged as India VIX spiked to a nine-month high after US-Israel strikes on Iran reignited West Asia tensions, sending benchmark indices tumbling and keeping investors on edge amid fears of higher crude prices, currency pressure and prolonged geopolitical uncertainty.

items.title: NHAI faces  ₹29k-cr hit as SC upholds extra compensation for highway landowners
items.summary: A bench of Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and Justice Ujjal Bhuyan reaffirmed that landowners whose land was acquired for highway projects between 1997 and 2008 are entitled to extra compensation, known as solatium, and interest, even for past acquisitions.&nbsp;

items.title: Auto Stocks to Buy: CLSA sees up to 36% gains in two shares – Are they in your portfolio?
items.summary: CLSA has turned bullish on auto stocks, highlighting Mahindra & Mahindra (M&M) and Bajaj Auto as top picks. The brokerage has assigned an Accumulate rating on both stocks, with upside potential of up to 36 per cent.

items.summary: TVS Holdings has declared an interim dividend for the financial year ending on March 31, 2026. Check dividend rate, record date and other details.

items.summary: Puravankara Limited plans to launch 30 new housing projects across South and West India. These projects are expected to generate an estimated revenue of Rs 35,636 crore over the next two and a half years. The company is confident in rising residential property demand. This expansion aims to tap into the growing market and strengthen its presence in key areas.

items.title: Waaree Energies shares gain over 3.5% as board approves  ₹3,900 crore capex for glass manufacturing unit
items.summary: Waaree Energies shares rose 3.6% to  ₹3,197 after approving a  ₹3,900 crore capital expenditure for a glass manufacturing plant. The company also increased its stake in Waaree Transpower from 64.04% to 75.10%.&nbsp;

items.title: BKT ramps up IPL push as it pivots to consumer market
items.summary: BKT Tyres is boosting its Indian Premier League presence. The company is moving from business-to-business exports to a consumer brand in India. BKT is partnering with eight IPL teams for wider visibility. This strategy supports their growth in the on-road tyre market. BKT aims for significant revenue by 2030.

items.title: Avantel bags Rs 460 crore contract for Indian Railways' real-time train tracking system
items.summary: Avantel has secured a significant contract worth Rs 459.9 crore. The company will supply and maintain Real-Time Train Information System devices for Indian Railways. This project, developed with ISRO, uses satellite technology for train tracking. The deal covers 12000 devices over three years. It aims to enhance real-time tracking and improve railway operations efficiency.

items.title: India’s youngest infrastructure financier looks beyond debt
items.summary: State-owned National Bank for Financing Infrastructure and Development plans to invest in underserved sectors such as urban infrastructure, waste management, and smaller healthcare projects, areas where capital is harder to access.

items.title: Truckers invoke force majeure amid fuel shortage, decline in cargo movement
items.summary: The industry body also called for immediate clearance of overdue payments and ad hoc financial support to ease a mounting liquidity crunch among fleet operators

items.summary: United Spirits’ Rs 16,660 crore sale of its RCB stake has been termed value accretive by brokerages, which expect improved capital allocation and potential shareholder returns. The deal, involving major investors, sets a strong valuation benchmark for IPL franchises and could act as a positive trigger for the stock.

items.title: No fuel shortage, say oil firms; urge public to avoid panic buying
items.summary: Indian Oil Corporation, Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Ltd said there is no shortage of petrol, diesel or LPG in the country and urged people to avoid panic buying amid rumours. The companies said fuel outlets are well-stocked and supply chains remain stable, even as the West Asia conflict has disrupted global energy flows.

items.summary: Jaipur-based Zuvelio scales as a profitable, digital-first lab-grown diamond brand, specialising in durable, personalised rings for a global market.

items.summary: Indian equities are experiencing a sharp correction due to geopolitical tensions, with valuations becoming attractive. However, elevated oil prices and conflict duration pose significant risks, potentially impacting trade deficits and currencies. Investors are advised to focus on reasonably valued stocks, with engineering, EPC, and IT midcaps showing promise.

items.title: Are commodity markets MCX and NCDEX closed on March 26, 2026 for Shri Ram Navami? Check 2026 holiday list
items.summary: MCX will remain closed in the morning session on March 26, 2026, for Shri Ram Navami, while NCDEX will be shut for the entire day. Equity markets will also remain closed. Trading on MCX will resume in the evening session, as per the exchange’s 2026 holiday calendar.

items.title: India’s economic rise has been impressive but growth could stall if we let deep disparities persist
items.summary: India’s economy is growing rapidly, but inclusive prosperity remains elusive. With inequality entrenched and even the middle class under strain, the challenge is not just to sustain growth, but ensure that its gains are shared widely.

items.title: True Story Films and Collective Studios Announce ‘Khana Dil Se’, India’s First AI-Powered Food Show

Created by Hansal Mehta, the show marks his return to food storytelling decades after Khana Khazana. It features chef Shamsher Ahmed as culinary consultant, bringing expertise in regional cuisines.

items.title: India must channel $10 trillion in household gold into financial system: Former minister Choudhary
items.summary: India's vast household gold reserves could boost economic growth. Experts suggest shifting consumers to financial instruments like Electronic Gold Receipts. This move aims to reduce reliance on gold imports and ease pressure on the current account deficit. The National Stock Exchange is ready with platforms and technology to facilitate this transition.

items.title: Why SBI Life is winning the valuation game vs HDFC, ICICI Prudential
items.summary: SBI Life has outperformed peers on growth and valuation, but its heavy reliance on ULIPs could test momentum if markets remain weak in FY27.

items.title: CMPDI IPO allotment date likely today. GMP, steps to check share allotment status online

items.title: L&T shares jump 4%, up nearly 10% in two days. What’s triggering this uptick?

Despite a 15% fall amid Middle East tensions, operations remain largely unaffected with 95% projects running. West Asia continues to be key, contributing nearly 40% to the order book and 75% to international business.

items.title: Drill India drill: Iran war triggers India’s boldest hunt for oil
items.summary: The Iran war has pushed countries to prioritise energy self-reliance, with India accelerating an already underway shift toward boosting domestic production. The move is not new but builds on policy momentum from 2025, now fast-tracked due to geopolitical risks and supply disruptions.

items.title: India buys 60 million barrels of Russian oil for April
items.summary: Indian refiners are boosting Russian oil imports by 60 million barrels next month, a significant jump to counter Middle East supply disruptions. This move, facilitated by a US waiver, sees major players like Mangalore Refinery returning to Russian crude. The nation is also increasing Venezuelan oil purchases, as Russia enjoys renewed export profits.

items.title: Nifty can fall below 19,000 in worst case GFC-like scenario, warns Bernstein; rupee could breach 110/USD

items.title: GameStop posts 14% fall in quarterly revenue amid digital gaming shift

items.title: India VIX today near 25. Here's what it signals for the Indian stock market
